title: Telephone numbers in Belgium

is a sequence of nine or ten digits dialed on a telephone to make a call on the Belgian telephone network. Belgium is under a [full number dialing plan](/wiki/Telephone_numbering_plan#Full-number_dialing "Telephone numbering plan"), meaning that the full national number must be dialed for all calls, while it retains the trunk code, '0', for all national dialling.
for landlines with the mobile numbers prefix **045**, **046,** **047,** **048,** or **049**. But landlines in Liège do not start with those sequences.

in [Europe](/wiki/Europe "Europe") and **011** in [North America](/wiki/North_America "North America")), followed by 32 (the country code for Belgium), then the area code minus the trunk code '0', and finally the local number.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Telephone numbers in Belgium

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Country | [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium") |
| Continent | [Europe](/wiki/Telephone_numbers_in_Europe "Telephone numbers in Europe") |
| Numbering plan type | [Open](/wiki/Telephone_numbering_plan "Telephone numbering plan") |
| Format | Geographical: 0x xxx xx xx 0xx xx xx xx Mobile: 045x xx xx xx thru 049x xx xx xx |
| [Country code](/wiki/Telephone_country_code "Telephone country code") | 32 |
| [International access](/wiki/International_call_prefix "International call prefix") | 00 |
| [Long-distance](/wiki/Trunk_prefix "Trunk prefix") | 0 |

**A [telephone number](/wiki/Telephone_number "Telephone number") in [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium")** is a sequence of nine or ten digits dialed on a telephone to make a call on the Belgian telephone network. Belgium is under a [full number dialing plan](/wiki/Telephone_numbering_plan#Full-number_dialing "Telephone numbering plan"), meaning that the full national number must be dialed for all calls, while it retains the trunk code, '0', for all national dialling.

Exception: Some "special services" use 3 or 4 digits with no area or trunk codes, e.g.: *112* and *100* (fire brigade and ambulance); *101* (police); *1307* (info in French) or *1207* (info in Dutch), etc.

"[112](/wiki/112_(emergency_telephone_number) "112 (emergency telephone number)")" is an emergency number for contacting the fire brigade, ambulance and police in all 27 countries of the European Union. Operators will help the caller in the country's native language, in English, or the language of any neighbouring country.
Calls to this number for contacting the police are forwarded to "101", losing response time.

The [telephone numbering plan](/wiki/Telephone_numbering_plan "Telephone numbering plan") allows for numbers have varying lengths (9 digits for [landline](/wiki/Landline "Landline") numbers, and 10 digits for [mobile numbers](/wiki/Mobile_phone "Mobile phone")).

## Overview and structure

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Telephone_numbers_in_Belgium&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Overview and structure")]

[Area codes](/wiki/Area_codes "Area codes") in Belgium are, excluding the leading '0', one or two digits long. Numbers are of variable length; landlines have a seven-digit subscriber number and a one-digit area code for large cities, while smaller cities have a six-digit subscriber number and a two-digit area code. All Belgian telephone numbers dialed within Belgium must use the leading '0' trunk code. Area codes are separated from the subscriber number by a slash and a space, and subscriber number digits are in the format *xxx xx xx* or *xx xx xx* (sometimes *xxx xxx*), depending on the length of the area code

Examples

```
  0x xxx xx xx - dialing a big city, such as Brussels, Antwerp, Liège and Ghent.
 0xx  xx xx xx - dialing a small city, such as Kortrijk, Mons, Ostend, Aalst or Verviers
04xx  xx xx xx - dialing a mobile number from a landline or another mobile phone.
```

Remark that Liège shares the trunk number **04** for landlines with the mobile numbers prefix **045**, **046,** **047,** **048,** or **049**. But landlines in Liège do not start with those sequences.

From outside Belgium, a caller dials their international call prefix (typically **00** in [Europe](/wiki/Europe "Europe") and **011** in [North America](/wiki/North_America "North America")), followed by 32 (the country code for Belgium), then the area code minus the trunk code '0', and finally the local number.

```
Dialing from New York to Brussels
011-32-2-555-12-12 - Omitting the leading "0".
```

```
Dialing from New York to Charleroi
011-32-71-123-456 - The subscriber number shortens with the addition of a number to the area code.
```

```
Dialing from New York to a mobile number
011-32-4xx-12-34-56 - The dialer omits the leading "0".
```

Mobile/GSM area codes always begin with *04xx* and the subscriber number is six digits long. Numbers are usually provided by [Orange](/wiki/Orange_Belgium "Orange Belgium") (formerly [Mobistar](/wiki/Mobistar "Mobistar")), [Base](/wiki/Base_(mobile_telephony_provider) "Base (mobile telephony provider)"), or [Proximus](/wiki/Proximus "Proximus"), and more recently by [Telenet](/wiki/Telenet_(Belgium) "Telenet (Belgium)") as well. Each provider has a unique number assigned as the second digit in the area code: Proximus numbers begin with *047x* or *0460*, Base numbers with *048x*, Orange numbers with *049x* and Telenet numbers with *0467* and *0468*. With the introduction of [number portability](/wiki/Telephone_number_portability_(disambiguation) "Telephone number portability (disambiguation)"), both for landlines and mobile numbers, area codes may no longer correspond with their original providers or [local switches](/wiki/Telephone_exchange "Telephone exchange").

## Landlines

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Telephone_numbers_in_Belgium&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Landlines")]

* *010:* [Wavre](/wiki/Wavre "Wavre") (*Waver*)
* *011:* [Hasselt](/wiki/Hasselt "Hasselt")
* *012:* [Tongeren](/wiki/Tongeren "Tongeren") (*Tongres*)
* *013:* [Diest](/wiki/Diest "Diest")
* *014:* [Geel](/wiki/Geel "Geel"), [Herentals](/wiki/Herentals "Herentals"), [Turnhout](/wiki/Turnhout "Turnhout")
* *015:* [Mechelen](/wiki/Mechelen "Mechelen") (*Malines*)
* *016:* [Leuven](/wiki/Leuven "Leuven") (*Louvain*), [Tienen](/wiki/Tienen "Tienen") (*Tirlemont*)
* *019:* [Waremme](/wiki/Waremme "Waremme") (*Borgworm*)
* *02:* [Brussels](/wiki/Brussels "Brussels") (*Bruxelles*/*Brussel*)
* *03:* [Antwerp](/wiki/Antwerp "Antwerp") (*Antwerpen*/*Anvers*), [Sint-Niklaas](/wiki/Sint-Niklaas "Sint-Niklaas")
* *04:* [Liège](/wiki/Li%C3%A8ge "Liège") (*Luik*), [Voeren](/wiki/Voeren "Voeren") (*Fourons*)
* *050:* [Bruges](/wiki/Bruges "Bruges") (*Brugge*), [Zeebrugge](/wiki/Zeebrugge "Zeebrugge")
* *051:* [Roeselare](/wiki/Roeselare "Roeselare") (*Roulers*)
* *052:* [Dendermonde](/wiki/Dendermonde "Dendermonde") (*Termonde*)
* *053:* [Aalst](/wiki/Aalst,_Belgium "Aalst, Belgium") (*Alost*)
* *054:* [Ninove](/wiki/Ninove "Ninove")
* *055:* [Ronse](/wiki/Ronse "Ronse") (*Renaix*)
* *056:* [Kortrijk](/wiki/Kortrijk "Kortrijk") (*Courtrai*), [Comines-Warneton](/wiki/Comines-Warneton "Comines-Warneton") (*Komen-Waasten*), [Mouscron](/wiki/Mouscron "Mouscron") (*Moeskroen*)
* *057:* [Ypres](/wiki/Ypres "Ypres") (*Ieper*)
* *058:* [Veurne](/wiki/Veurne "Veurne") (*Furnes*)
* *059:* [Ostend](/wiki/Ostend "Ostend"), [Bredene](/wiki/Bredene "Bredene"), [Gistel](/wiki/Gistel "Gistel") (*Oostende*/*Ostende*, *Bredene*, *Gistel*)
* *060:* [Chimay](/wiki/Chimay "Chimay")
* *061:* [Bastogne](/wiki/Bastogne "Bastogne") (*Bastenaken*), [Libramont-Chevigny](/wiki/Libramont-Chevigny "Libramont-Chevigny")
* *063:* [Arlon](/wiki/Arlon "Arlon") (*Aarlen*)
* *064:* [La Louvière](/wiki/La_Louvi%C3%A8re "La Louvière")
* *065:* [Mons](/wiki/Mons,_Belgium "Mons, Belgium") (*Bergen*), [Casteau](/wiki/Casteau "Casteau")
* *067:* [Nivelles](/wiki/Nivelles "Nivelles") (*Nijvel*), [Soignies](/wiki/Soignies "Soignies") (*Zinnik*)
* *068:* [Ath](/wiki/Ath "Ath") (*Aat*)
* *069:* [Tournai](/wiki/Tournai "Tournai") (*Doornik*)
* *071:* [Charleroi](/wiki/Charleroi "Charleroi")
* *080:* [Stavelot](/wiki/Stavelot "Stavelot")
* *081:* [Namur](/wiki/Namur_(city) "Namur (city)") (*Namen*)
* *082:* [Dinant](/wiki/Dinant "Dinant")
* *083:* [Ciney](/wiki/Ciney "Ciney")
* *084:* [Marche-en-Famenne](/wiki/Marche-en-Famenne "Marche-en-Famenne")
* *085:* [Huy](/wiki/Huy "Huy") (*Hoei*)
* *086:* [Durbuy](/wiki/Durbuy "Durbuy")
* *087:* [Verviers](/wiki/Verviers "Verviers")
* *089:* [Genk](/wiki/Genk "Genk")
* *09:* [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent") (*Gent*/*Gand*)

Landlines in Liège have numbers starting with 04, and so do mobile phones all over the country. But mobile numbers are 04xx xx xx xx – 10 digits in total – while Liège numbers are 04 xxx xx xx – 9 digits in total. Landlines in Liège also never starts with 04 6x, 04 7x, 04 8x or 04 9x.

## Mobile numbers

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Telephone_numbers_in_Belgium&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Mobile numbers")]

Subscriber numbers may have been ported to another network, due to [mobile number portability](/wiki/Mobile_number_portability "Mobile number portability").

* *0455:* mobile phone, Range is owned by [Orange](/wiki/Orange_S.A. "Orange S.A.") (for their brand VOO).
* *0456:* mobile phone, Range is owned by [Proximus](/wiki/Proximus "Proximus") (for their brand Mobile Viking).
* *0460:* mobile phone, Range is owned by [Proximus](/wiki/Proximus "Proximus").
* *0465:* mobile phone, Range is owned by [Lycamobile](/wiki/Lycamobile "Lycamobile").
* *0466:* mobile phone, Range is owned by [Orange](/wiki/Orange_S.A. "Orange S.A.") (for their brand Hey!).
* *0467:* mobile phone, Range is owned by [Liberty Global](/wiki/Liberty_Global "Liberty Global") (for their brand Telenet).
* *0468:* mobile phone, Range is owned by [Liberty Global](/wiki/Liberty_Global "Liberty Global") (for their brand Telenet).
* *047x:* mobile phone, Range is owned by [Proximus](/wiki/Proximus "Proximus").
* *048x:* mobile phone, Range is owned by [Liberty Global](/wiki/Liberty_Global "Liberty Global") (for their brand BASE).
* *049x:* mobile phone, Range is owned by [Orange](/wiki/Orange_S.A. "Orange S.A.") (for their brand Orange Belgium).

On some mobile phones, the caller ID may fail unless the leading *0* is replaced with a *+* and the country code, i.e. a caller's number *0474 12 34 56* might need to be manually replaced to *+32 474 12 34 56* in your phone. More ranges have become available between 0440 and 0468. For the latest list refer to the BIPT reference.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Non-geographic numbers

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Telephone_numbers_in_Belgium&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Non-geographic numbers")]

* *045x* Premium numbers (€1.00/min).
* *070*: Premium pay rate services (€0.30/min.)
* *077*: [Machine to machine](/wiki/Machine_to_machine "Machine to machine") communication[[2]](#cite_note-2)
* *078*: National pay rate services.
* *0800*: [toll free](/wiki/Toll_free "Toll free") €0.00 call rate services.
* *090x*  - Premium pay rate numbers\*:
  + 0900 (€0.50/min.).
  + 0901 (€0.50/call).
  + 0902 (€1.00/min.).
  + 0903 (€1.50/min.).
  + 0904 (€2.00/min.).
  + 0905 (€2.00/call).
  + 0906 (€1.00/min.).
  + 0907 (€2.00/min.).
  + 0909 (€31.00/call).

Number format:

```
07x/xxx.xxx 
0800/xx.xxx
090x/xx.xxx
```

Archived from [the original](http://www.bipt.be/en/operators/telecommunication/Numbering/regulation/decision-of-the-bipt-council-of-6-september-2011-on-the-determination-of-the-numbering-plan-for-m2m-communication) on 2017-02-19.
